about: Canara Bank was merged with erstwhile Syndicate Bank in FY21. Canara was incorporated in 1906 and nationalised in 1969, along with 13 other major commercial banks of India, by the GoI. key_points.raw:
ABOUT [ edit ]

Canara Bank was merged with erstwhile Syndicate Bank in FY21. Canara was incorporated in 1906 and nationalised in 1969, along with 13 other major commercial banks of India, by the GoI. The bank is headquartered in Bangalore.Canara Bank was merged with erstwhile Syndicate Bank (e-SB) on April 1, 2020.

KEY POINTS [ edit ]

Ratios as of Q3 FY26[1][2][3]
Net Interest Margin - 2.50%
Gross NPA - 2.08%
Net NPA - 0.45%
CASA Ratio - 29.52%
PCR: 94.19%
Cost to Income: 46.86%
Cost of Funds: 5.18%

Market Share[4]
The bank has a market share of 6.2% and 6.5% in net advances and total deposits, respectively as on Jun'23

Branch Network Q3 FY26[5]
Presently, the bank operates 10,066 branches, 12,000 BC Points, 7,048 ATMs and 3,740 Recycler across India. It also operates 4 international branches.
32% in Rural, 30% in Semi-Urban, 19% in Urban & 19% in Metro.

Loan Book Q3 FY26[6]
Gross advances stands at Rs 11,92,326 Cr
Presently, corporate loans account for 41% of total loan book, followed by Agriculture (23%), retail (23%) and MSME (13%).

Corporate Book Exposure[7]
Presently, the total size of bank's corporate book is Rs. 4,88,285 Crs.
Top industries to which the bank's corporate book is exposed to are
NBFCs: ~37%,
Infrastructure: ~33%
Commercial Real Estate: ~6%
Textile: ~5%
Iron & Steel: ~4%
Engineering: ~4%
Food Processing: ~3%
Petroleum, Coal products & Nuclear Fuels: ~3%
Construction: ~2%
Chemicals & Chemical Products: ~3%

Geographical Revenue Segmentation[6]
Domestic : ~93%
Overseas : ~7%

International Business[8]
The Bank has 4 overseas branches, viz., London (UK), New York (USA), Dubai International Financial Center - DIFC (UAE) and IBU GIFT CITY Gandhinagar, Gujarat. Besides the above 4 branches, the Bank has a Representative Office at Sharjah (UAE) and a WoS; Canara Bank (Tanzania) Ltd.
Bank has also progressed towards divestment / winding up of Canara Bank (Tanzania) Ltd., which is expected to be completed in FY25.

Gujarat International Financial Tec City (GIFT City)[9] Branch at Ahmedabad, Gujarat has been inaugurated to facilitate customers with Forex Transactions, Money Market Deals and Security Investments other than various corporate services.

Significant Subsidiaries Operations[10]
The Bank has presence in various related business through its subsidiaries:-

1. Canara Robeco Asset Management Co Ltd (38% stake)[11] - It is a JV of Canara Bank and M/sOrix Corporation, Japan which handles 24 mutual fund schemes including Gold exchange traded fund.

2. Canara Bank Securities Ltd[11] - It is a WoS of bank which offers Demat Services, Online Trading, e-Insurance and distribution of Financial products to both institutional and retail clients.

3. Canara HSBC Life Insurance Company Ltd (36.5% stake)[11] - It is a Life Insurance Joint Venture floated by the Bank in association with HSBC Insurance (Asia Pacific) Holding Ltd. and Punjab National Bank in the year 2007.

4. CanFin Homes Ltd (~30% stake)[11] - It is one of the premier housing finance entities in the country.

5. Canbank Factors Ltd.(70% stake)[12]

IPO Updates[10]
Following the listing of Canara Robeco and Canara HSBC in October 2025, the bank reduced its stake through an OFS in the IPOs, bringing shareholding down from 51% to 38% and 36.5% respectively, resulting in both entities transitioning from subsidiaries to associates. The transaction generated a net gain of ₹1,930 Cr after accounting for ₹76 Cr in IPO-related expenses.

Outlook[3]
The bank has guided for ~10.5% business growth for FY26, with advances growth of 10–11% and deposit growth of 9–10%, while targeting a CASA ratio of 32% and NIM of 2.75–2.80%. On asset quality, management expects GNPA at 2.5%, NNPA at 0.6%, PCR at 93%, with slippage ratio and credit cost at 0.9% each. Profitability guidance includes RoE of 18.5%, RoA of 1.05%, and EPS of ₹19 (FV ₹2).

KMP Change[13][14]
Shri K. Satyanarayana Raju, ceased to be the MD & CEO of the Bank w.e.f. close of business hours on 31st Dec 2025. Hardeep Singh Ahluwalia has been appointed as MD and CEO effective 1st Jan 2026.
